title: Popups and Ads
drDigitalIntro: Popups are windows that suddenly appear on websites — some are scams. Ads are how many sites make money, but some pretend to be download buttons or security warnings. A popup that says 'VIRUS DETECTED!' or 'Your computer is infected!' is ALWAYS fake. Never click it — just close it with its X. Real virus warnings never appear inside a web page.

playgroundTask.goal: Close a scam popup without clicking it

steps.say: Go to freegames.example — type it and press Enter. Careful: sketchy sites throw popups at you.
A scary popup claims your computer has a virus. It's FAKE — never click inside it. Click the ✕ in the corner to close it.

drDigitalSuccess: You closed the fake popup without touching it. A browser popup claiming you have a virus is always a scam — close it with the ✕ every time.
drDigitalHint: Don't click the red CLEAN NOW button — that's the trap. Click the small ✕ in the top corner of the popup.
